title: ipomopsis aggregata var. arizonica
intro: Ipomopsis aggregata var. arizonica, also known as Arizona Globe Mallow, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant contains bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The Arizona Globe Mallow has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, fever, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
